message.content:
Search the codebase thoroughly for any existing definitions or lemmas related to:
1. Hypergraphs (r-uniform, uniform)
2. Matchings in hypergraphs or graphs (pairwise disjoint edges)
3. Finset-based graph representations
4. Nat.choose / binomial coefficients usage in conjecture statements
5. sSup used to define maximal quantities over finsets

Look in both FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/ and FormalConjectures/Util/ and any Mathlib-related imports. Report file paths, line numbers, and the relevant definitions. I need to know if IsRUniform, HasMatching, or maxEdgesNoMatching (or equivalents) are defined elsewhere.

    26‚Üí
    27‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} [Fintype őĪ] [DecidableEq őĪ]
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚ąß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚ĄĚ)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := Fintype.card őĪ
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset őĪ) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set őĪ))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset őĪ) : ‚Ą§ := (s.card : ‚Ą§) - (‚čÉ v ‚ąą (s : Set őĪ), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚ĄĚ)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚ąß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : őĪ => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚ąß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚ĄĚ)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚ĄĚ)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    91‚Üí  (‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚ĄĚ¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚ĄĚ¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚ąÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚ąß (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.IsPath) ‚ąß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚ąě` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   129‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1020
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíConjecture on the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices
    23‚Üícontaining no matching of size $k$ (the ErdŇĎs Matching Conjecture).
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1020](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1020)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er65d] ErdŇĎs, P., *A problem on independent r-tuples*, Ann. Univ. Sci. Budapest. E√∂tv√∂s Sect.
    28‚ÜíMath. 8 (1965), 93‚Äď95.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Topics in combinatorial analysis*, Proc. Second Louisiana Conf. on
    31‚ÜíCombinatorics, Graph Theory and Computing (1971), 2‚Äď20.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Finset
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos1020
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on vertex set `Fin n`: every edge has exactly $r$ vertices. -/
    39‚Üídef IsRUniform {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = r
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A matching of size $k$ in a hypergraph: $k$ pairwise vertex-disjoint edges. -/
    43‚Üídef HasMatching {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    44‚Üí  ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), M ‚äÜ H ‚ąß M.card = k ‚ąß
    45‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą M, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą M, e‚āĀ ‚Č† e‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint e‚āĀ e‚āā
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- `maxEdgesNoMatching n r k` is the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph
    48‚Üíon $n$ vertices that contains no matching of size $k$. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def maxEdgesNoMatching (n r k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    50‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    51‚Üí    IsRUniform H r ‚ąß ¬¨HasMatching H k ‚ąß H.card = m}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1020 (ErdŇĎs Matching Conjecture) [Er65d, Er71, p.103]:
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíFor all $r \geq 3$, the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices
    57‚Üícontaining no matching of size $k$ equals
    58‚Üí$$
    59‚Üí  \max\left(\binom{rk-1}{r},\; \binom{n}{r} - \binom{n-k+1}{r}\right).
    60‚Üí$$
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_1020 (n r k : ‚Ąē) (hr : r ‚Č• 3) (hk : k ‚Č• 1) :
    64‚Üí    maxEdgesNoMatching n r k =
    65‚Üí      max (Nat.choose (r * k - 1) r) (Nat.choose n r - Nat.choose (n - k + 1) r) := by
    66‚Üí  sorry
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend Erdos1020
    69‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 719
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/719](https://www.erdosproblems.com/719)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA conjecture of ErdŇĎs and Sauer.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[Er81] ErdŇĎs, P., *On the combinatorial problems which I would most like to see solved*, 1981.
    27‚Üí-/
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üíopen Finset
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üínamespace Erdos719
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of $r$-element subsets. -/
    34‚Üídef IsRUniformHypergraph (n r : ‚Ąē) (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    35‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = r
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph is $K_{r+1}^r$-free: no $(r+1)$-element vertex set has
    38‚Üíall its $r$-element subsets as edges. -/
    39‚Üídef IsHypergraphCliqueFree (r : ‚Ąē) {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  ¬¨‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = r + 1 ‚ąß Finset.powersetCard r S ‚äÜ H
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- The Tur√°n number $\mathrm{ex}_r(n; K_{r+1}^r)$: the maximum number of edges in an
    43‚Üí$r$-uniform $K_{r+1}^r$-free hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    44‚Üínoncomputable def turanHypergraphNumber (n r : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    46‚Üí    IsRUniformHypergraph n r H ‚ąß H.card = k ‚ąß IsHypergraphCliqueFree r H}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The edges of a decomposition piece: if $S$ has $r$ elements (a $K_r^r$ copy),
    49‚Üíit contributes the single edge $\{S\}$; if $S$ has $r+1$ elements (a $K_{r+1}^r$ copy),
    50‚Üíit contributes all $r$-element subsets of $S$. -/
    51‚Üídef pieceEdges (r : ‚Ąē) {n : ‚Ąē} (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Finset (Finset (Fin n)) :=
    52‚Üí  if S.card = r then {S}
    53‚Üí  else if S.card = r + 1 then Finset.powersetCard r S
    54‚Üí  else ‚ąÖ
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 719 (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSauer conjecture) [Er81]:
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíIs every $r$-uniform hypergraph $G$ on $n$ vertices the union of at most
    60‚Üí$\mathrm{ex}_r(n; K_{r+1}^r)$ edge-disjoint copies of $K_r^r$ (single edges) and $K_{r+1}^r$
    61‚Üí(complete $(r+1)$-cliques)?
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_719 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ (n r : ‚Ąē) (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))),
    65‚Üí    IsRUniformHypergraph n r H ‚Üí
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (pieces : Finset (Finset (Fin n))),
    67‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą pieces, S.card = r ‚ą® S.card = r + 1) ‚ąß
    68‚Üí      (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, ‚ąÉ S ‚ąą pieces, e ‚ąą pieceEdges r S) ‚ąß
    69‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą pieces, ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą pieceEdges r S, e ‚ąą H) ‚ąß
    70‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S‚āĀ ‚ąą pieces, ‚ąÄ S‚āā ‚ąą pieces, S‚āĀ ‚Č† S‚āā ‚Üí
    71‚Üí        Disjoint (pieceEdges r S‚āĀ) (pieceEdges r S‚āā)) ‚ąß
    72‚Üí      pieces.card ‚Č§ turanHypergraphNumber n r := by
    73‚Üí  sorry
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üíend Erdos719
    76‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 747
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/747](https://www.erdosproblems.com/747)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíShamir's problem (1979): How large should $\ell(n)$ be such that, almost surely, a random
    25‚Üí$3$-uniform hypergraph on $3n$ vertices with $\ell(n)$ edges must contain $n$ vertex-disjoint
    26‚Üíedges?
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[JKV08] Johansson, A., Kahn, J., and Vu, V., _Factors in random graphs_, Random Structures
    29‚Üí& Algorithms 33 (2008), 1-28.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Ka23] Kahn, J., _Asymptotics for Shamir's problem_, Annals of Mathematics 198 (2023), 1-69.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    35‚Üíopen Finset
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos747
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The set of all $3$-element subsets of $\operatorname{Fin} N$ (potential hyperedges). -/
    40‚Üídef threeEdges (N : ‚Ąē) : Finset (Finset (Fin N)) :=
    41‚Üí  Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s => s.card = 3)
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- The set of all $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $\operatorname{Fin} N$ with exactly $m$ edges. -/
    44‚Üídef hypergraphsWithEdges (N m : ‚Ąē) : Finset (Finset (Finset (Fin N))) :=
    45‚Üí  (threeEdges N).powerset.filter (fun H => H.card = m)
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph $H$ contains a matching of size $k$: there exist
    48‚Üí    $k$ pairwise vertex-disjoint edges in $H$. -/
    49‚Üídef HasMatching {N : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin N))) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Finset (Fin N)), M ‚äÜ H ‚ąß M.card = k ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą M, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą M, e‚āĀ ‚Č† e‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint e‚āĀ e‚āā
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The fraction of $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $\operatorname{Fin} N$ with $m$ edges that
    54‚Üí    contain a matching of size $k$. Returns $0$ if no such hypergraphs exist. -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def hypergraphMatchingFraction (N m k : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    56‚Üí  ((hypergraphsWithEdges N m).filter (fun H => HasMatching H k)).card /
    57‚Üí  ((hypergraphsWithEdges N m).card : ‚ĄĚ)
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 747 (Shamir's problem):
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, almost surely a random $3$-uniform hypergraph on $3n$ vertices
    63‚Üíwith at least $(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n$ edges contains $n$ vertex-disjoint edges.
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíFormally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, there exists $n_0$ such that for all
    66‚Üí$n \geq n_0$ and all $m$ with $(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n \leq m \leq \binom{3n}{3}$,
    67‚Üíthe fraction of $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $3n$ vertices with $m$ edges containing a perfect
    68‚Üímatching is at least $1 - \delta$.
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíProved by Johansson, Kahn, and Vu [JKV08]; sharp threshold by Kahn [Ka23].
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_747 :
    74‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    75‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őī : ‚ĄĚ, őī > 0 ‚Üí
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÉ n‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• n‚āÄ ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      ‚ąÄ m : ‚Ąē, (m : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (1 + őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) * Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí
    78‚Üí        m ‚Č§ (3 * n).choose 3 ‚Üí
    79‚Üí        hypergraphMatchingFraction (3 * n) m n ‚Č• 1 - őī := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üíend Erdos747
    83‚Üí

    37‚Üí/-- A hypergraph on vertex type $V$, represented as a finset of edges,
    38‚Üíwhere each edge is a finset of vertices. -/
    39‚Üístructure Hypergraph (V : Type*) [DecidableEq V] where

    42‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is $r$-uniform if every edge has exactly $r$ vertices. -/
    43‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsUniform {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=

    46‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of a hypergraph: no edge with $\geq 2$ vertices is monochromatic. -/
    47‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)

    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- A hypergraph has chromatic number exactly $k$ if it can be properly colored

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $k \geq 2$ and $A_k \subseteq [0,1]$ be the set of $\alpha$ such that there exists some
    25‚Üí$\beta(\alpha) > \alpha$ with the property that, if $G_1, G_2, \ldots$ is a sequence of $k$-uniform
    26‚Üíhypergraphs with
    27‚Üí$$\liminf \frac{e(G_n)}{\binom{|G_n|}{k}} > \alpha$$
    28‚Üíthen there exist subgraphs $H_n \subseteq G_n$ such that $|H_n| \to \infty$ and
    29‚Üí$$\liminf \frac{e(H_n)}{\binom{|H_n|}{k}} > \beta,$$
    30‚Üíand further that this property does not necessarily hold if $> \alpha$ is
    31‚Üíreplaced by $\geq \alpha$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíIt is known that $A_2 = \{1 - 1/m : m \geq 1\}$ (ErdŇĎs-Stone-Simonovits).

    74‚Üí          (G i).density ‚Č• őĪ + őī) ‚Üí

    77‚Üí          (‚ąÄ M : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, i ‚Č• N ‚Üí subSizes i ‚Č• M) ‚ąß
    78‚Üí          (‚ąÄ i, ‚ąÉ f : Fin (subSizes i) ‚Ü™ Fin (sizes i),
    79‚Üí            (H i).IsSubgraphVia (G i) f) ‚ąß
    80‚Üí          (‚ąÉ őī' : ‚ĄĚ, őī' > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āĀ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, i ‚Č• N‚āĀ ‚Üí
    81‚Üí            (H i).density ‚Č• ő≤ + őī')) ‚ąß

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 616
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/616](https://www.erdosproblems.com/616)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r \geq 3$. For an $r$-uniform hypergraph $G$ let $\tau(G)$ denote the covering number
    25‚Üí(or transversal number), the minimum size of a set of vertices which includes
    26‚Üíat least one from each edge in $G$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíDetermine the best possible $t$ such that, if $G$ is an $r$-uniform hypergraph
    29‚Üíwhere every subgraph $G'$ on at most $3r - 3$ vertices has $\tau(G') \leq 1$, we have
    30‚Üí$\tau(G) \leq t$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[EHT91] ErdŇĎs, P., Hajnal, A., and Tuza, Zs., _Local constraints ensuring small representing
    33‚Üísets_. J. Combin. Theory Ser. A 58 (1991), 78-84.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen Finset
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos616
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on vertex set `Fin n`. -/
    41‚Üístructure UniformHypergraph (n r : ‚Ąē) where
    42‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    43‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = r
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- A set $S$ of vertices is a transversal of hypergraph $G$ if it meets every edge. -/
    46‚Üídef IsTransversal {n r : ‚Ąē} (G : UniformHypergraph n r) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą G.edges, ‚ąÉ v ‚ąą S, v ‚ąą e
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The covering number $\tau(G)$: the minimum size of a transversal. -/
    50‚Üínoncomputable def coveringNumber {n r : ‚Ąē} (G : UniformHypergraph n r) : ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = k ‚ąß IsTransversal G S}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The subhypergraph of $G$ induced on vertex set $W$: edges of $G$ contained in $W$. -/
    54‚Üídef inducedSub {n r : ‚Ąē} (G : UniformHypergraph n r) (W : Finset (Fin n)) :
    55‚Üí    UniformHypergraph n r where
    56‚Üí  edges := G.edges.filter (¬∑ ‚äÜ W)
    57‚Üí  uniform := fun e he => G.uniform e (Finset.mem_filter.mp he).1
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/-- The local covering property: every induced subhypergraph on at most
    60‚Üí$3r - 3$ vertices has covering number at most $1$. -/
    61‚Üídef HasLocalCoveringProperty {n r : ‚Ąē} (G : UniformHypergraph n r) : Prop :=
    62‚Üí  ‚ąÄ W : Finset (Fin n), W.card ‚Č§ 3 * r - 3 ‚Üí coveringNumber (inducedSub G W) ‚Č§ 1
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 616 ‚ÄĒ Upper bound [EHT91]:
    66‚ÜíIf $G$ is an $r$-uniform hypergraph ($r \geq 3$) where every subhypergraph on at most
    67‚Üí$3r - 3$ vertices has covering number $\leq 1$, then $\tau(G) \leq \frac{1}{5}r$.
    68‚Üí-/
    69‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    70‚Üítheorem erdos_616 :
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÄ r : ‚Ąē, 3 ‚Č§ r ‚Üí
    72‚Üí    ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ G : UniformHypergraph n r,
    73‚Üí    HasLocalCoveringProperty G ‚Üí
    74‚Üí    (coveringNumber G : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 5 * (r : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    75‚Üí  sorry
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí/--
    78‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 616 ‚ÄĒ Lower bound [EHT91]:
    79‚ÜíFor every $r \geq 3$, there exists an $r$-uniform hypergraph $G$ satisfying the local
    80‚Üícovering property with $\tau(G) \geq \frac{3}{16}r + \frac{7}{8}$.

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 860
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/860](https://www.erdosproblems.com/860)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $h(n)$ be such that, for any $m \geq 1$, in the interval $(m, m + h(n))$ there
    25‚Üíexist distinct integers $a_i$ for $1 \leq i \leq \pi(n)$ such that $p_i \mid a_i$, where $p_i$
    26‚Üídenotes the $i$-th prime.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíEstimate $h(n)$.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Pomerance [ErPo80], who proved that
    31‚Üí$h(n) \ll n^{3/2} / (\log n)^{1/2}$.
    32‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Selfridge proved $h(n) > (3 - o(1))n$, and Ruzsa proved $h(n)/n \to \infty$.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[ErPo80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pomerance, C., *Matching the natural numbers up to n with distinct
    35‚Üímultiples in another interval*, Nederl. Akad. Wetensch. Indag. Math. (1980).
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen Filter
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos860
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A prime covering of $(m, m + h)$ for primes up to $n$: an injective assignment
    43‚Üíof distinct integers in $(m, m + h)$, one for each prime $p \leq n$, such that
    44‚Üí$p$ divides the integer assigned to it. -/
    45‚Üídef HasPrimeCovering (n m h : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÉ a : {p : ‚Ąē // p.Prime ‚ąß p ‚Č§ n} ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    47‚Üí    Function.Injective a ‚ąß
    48‚Üí    (‚ąÄ p, m < a p ‚ąß a p < m + h) ‚ąß
    49‚Üí    (‚ąÄ p, p.val ‚ą£ a p)
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- `erdosPomeranceH n` is the smallest $h$ such that for every $m \geq 1$,
    52‚Üíin the open interval $(m, m + h)$ one can find $\pi(n)$ distinct multiples,
    53‚Üíone for each prime $p \leq n$. -/
    54‚Üínoncomputable def erdosPomeranceH (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    55‚Üí  sInf {h : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ m : ‚Ąē, m ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí HasPrimeCovering n m h}
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/--
    58‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 860, upper bound (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďPomerance [ErPo80]):
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíThere exists $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    61‚Üí$$h(n) \leq C \cdot n^{3/2} / (\log n)^{1/2}.$$
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_860 :
    65‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    66‚Üí      (erdosPomeranceH n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    67‚Üí        C * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((3 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) / (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 860, lower bound (Ruzsa):
    72‚Üí$h(n) / n \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$.
    73‚Üí-/
    74‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    75‚Üítheorem erdos_860.variants.lower_ruzsa :
    76‚Üí    Tendsto (fun n => (erdosPomeranceH n : ‚ĄĚ) / (n : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop atTop := by
    77‚Üí  sorry
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üíend Erdos860

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 710
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/710](https://www.erdosproblems.com/710)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ be minimal such that in $(n, n+f(n))$ there exist distinct integers
    25‚Üí$a_1, \ldots, a_n$ such that $k \mid a_k$ for all $1 \le k \le n$. The problem asks
    26‚Üíto obtain an asymptotic formula for $f(n)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Pomerance [ErPo80], who proved
    29‚Üí$$
    30‚Üí(2/\sqrt{e} + o(1)) \cdot n \cdot (\log n / \log \log n)^{1/2} \le f(n)
    31‚Üí\le (1.7398\ldots + o(1)) \cdot n \cdot (\log n)^{1/2}.
    32‚Üí$$
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíSee also [711].
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[ErPo80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pomerance, C., _Matching the natural numbers up to n with distinct multiples
    37‚Üíin another interval_. Nederl. Akad. Wetensch. Proc. Ser. A 83 (= Indag. Math. 42) (1980), 147-161.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Nat
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos710
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $f(n)$ for ErdŇĎs Problem 710: the minimal $f$ such that in the open interval
    45‚Üí$(n, n+f)$ there exist $n$ distinct integers $a_1, \ldots, a_n$ with $k \mid a_k$ for all
    46‚Üí$1 \le k \le n$. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def erdos710F (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  sInf {f : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ g : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    49‚Üí    Function.Injective g ‚ąß
    50‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i : Fin n, n < g i) ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i : Fin n, g i < n + f) ‚ąß
    52‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i : Fin n, (i.val + 1) ‚ą£ g i)}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 710: Obtain an asymptotic formula for $f(n)$, defined as the minimal $f$ such
    56‚Üíthat in $(n, n+f)$ there exist distinct integers $a_1, \ldots, a_n$ with $k \mid a_k$ for all
    57‚Üí$1 \le k \le n$. The answer is an unknown function $g$ such that $f(n) / g(n) \to 1$.
    58‚Üí-/
    59‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    60‚Üítheorem erdos_710 :
    61‚Üí    Filter.Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => (erdos710F n : ‚ĄĚ) / (answer(sorry) : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) n)
    62‚Üí      Filter.atTop (nhds 1) := by
    63‚Üí  sorry
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/--
    66‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 710, known lower bound [ErPo80]:
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \ge N_0$,
    69‚Üí$$
    70‚Üíf(n) \ge (2/\sqrt{e} - \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot (\log n / \log \log n)^{1/2}.
    71‚Üí$$
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_710.variants.lower :
    75‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      (erdos710F n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•
    78‚Üí        (2 / (Real.exp 1) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) - őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) *
    79‚Üí        (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ))) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí/--
    83‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 710, known upper bound [ErPo80]:
    84‚Üí
    85‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \ge N_0$,
    86‚Üí$$
    87‚Üíf(n) \le (1.7399 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot (\log n)^{1/2}.
    88‚Üí$$
    89‚Üí
    90‚ÜíThe constant $1.7398\ldots$ comes from the original paper; we use $1.7399$ as
    91‚Üía rational upper bound on that constant.
    92‚Üí-/
    93‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    94‚Üítheorem erdos_710.variants.upper :
    95‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    96‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    97‚Üí      (erdos710F n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    98‚Üí        (1.7399 + őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) * (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    99‚Üí  sorry

40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset őĪ) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set őĪ))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset őĪ) : ‚Ą§ := (s.card : ‚Ą§) - (‚čÉ v ‚ąą (s : Set őĪ), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚ĄĚ)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚ąß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : őĪ => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚ąß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚ĄĚ)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚ĄĚ)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    91‚Üí  (‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚ĄĚ¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚ĄĚ¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚ąÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚ąß (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.IsPath) ‚ąß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚ąě` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 593
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/593](https://www.erdosproblems.com/593)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíCharacterize those finite $3$-uniform hypergraphs which appear in every
    25‚Üí$3$-uniform hypergraph of chromatic number $> \aleph_0$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíSimilar problems were investigated by ErdŇĎs, Galvin, and Hajnal [EGH75].
    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs claims that for graphs the problem is completely solved: a graph of
    29‚Üíchromatic number $\geq \aleph_1$ must contain all finite bipartite graphs but need not
    30‚Üícontain any fixed odd cycle.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíWe define a finite $3$-uniform hypergraph $F$ to be *obligatory* if it appears
    33‚Üíin every $3$-uniform hypergraph of uncountable chromatic number. By analogy
    34‚Üíwith the graph case, we conjecture that the obligatory finite $3$-uniform
    35‚Üíhypergraphs are exactly the $2$-colorable ones.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[EGH75] ErdŇĎs, P., Galvin, F., and Hajnal, A.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[Er95d] ErdŇĎs, P.
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos593
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph on vertex type $V$: a collection of $3$-element
    45‚Üí    subsets of $V$. -/
    46‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (V : Type*) where
    47‚Üí  edges : Set (Finset V)
    48‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of a $3$-uniform hypergraph: no edge is monochromatic
    51‚Üí    (every edge contains two vertices receiving different colors). -/
    52‚Üídef Hypergraph3.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} (H : Hypergraph3 V)
    53‚Üí    {C : Type*} (c : V ‚Üí C) : Prop :=
    54‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÉ x ‚ąą e, ‚ąÉ y ‚ąą e, c x ‚Č† c y
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph has uncountable chromatic number: it admits no
    57‚Üí    proper coloring with countably many colors. -/
    58‚Üídef Hypergraph3.HasUncountableChromaticNumber {V : Type*}
    59‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3 V) : Prop :=
    60‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (C : Type*) [Countable C], ¬¨‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí C, H.IsProperColoring c
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- A finite $3$-uniform hypergraph $F$ on $W$ embeds into $H$ on $V$: there is an
    63‚Üí    injective map from $W$ to $V$ sending every edge of $F$ to an edge of $H$. -/
    64‚Üídef Hypergraph3.ContainsCopy {V W : Type*}
    65‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3 V) (F : Hypergraph3 W) : Prop :=
    66‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : W ‚Ü™ V, ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą F.edges, e.map f ‚ąą H.edges
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph is $2$-colorable: it admits a proper coloring with
    69‚Üí    two colors such that no edge is monochromatic. -/
    70‚Üídef Hypergraph3.Is2Colorable {V : Type*} (H : Hypergraph3 V) : Prop :=
    71‚Üí  ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Bool, H.IsProperColoring c
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/--
    74‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 593 [Er95d]:
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíA finite $3$-uniform hypergraph appears in every $3$-uniform hypergraph of
    77‚Üíuncountable chromatic number if and only if it is $2$-colorable.
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíThis is conjectured by analogy with the known graph result: a graph of
    80‚Üíchromatic number $\geq \aleph_1$ must contain all finite bipartite graphs but need not
    81‚Üícontain any fixed odd cycle [EGH75].
    82‚Üí-/
    83‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    84‚Üítheorem erdos_593 (W : Type*) [Fintype W]
    85‚Üí    (F : Hypergraph3 W) :
    86‚Üí    (‚ąÄ (V : Type*) (H : Hypergraph3 V),
    87‚Üí      H.HasUncountableChromaticNumber ‚Üí H.ContainsCopy F) ‚ÜĒ
    88‚Üí    F.Is2Colorable := by
    89‚Üí  sorry
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üíend Erdos593

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1024
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1024](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1024)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ be such that every $3$-uniform linear hypergraph on $n$ vertices contains
    25‚Üían independent set on $f(n)$ vertices.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA hypergraph is linear if $|A \cap B| \leq 1$ for all edges $A$ and $B$. An independent
    28‚Üíset of vertices is one which contains no edges. A $3$-uniform linear hypergraph
    29‚Üíis sometimes called a partial Steiner triple system.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíErdŇĎs proved $n^{1/2} \ll f(n) \ll n^{2/3}$. Phelps and R√∂dl [PhRo86] proved
    32‚Üí$f(n) \asymp (n \log n)^{1/2}$.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[PhRo86] Phelps, K. T. and R√∂dl, V., _Steiner triple systems with minimum independence
    35‚Üínumber_. Ars Combin. 21 (1986), 167‚Äď172.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen Finset
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos1024
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of $3$-element subsets. -/
    43‚Üístructure Hypergraph3Uniform (n : ‚Ąē) where
    44‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    45‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph is *linear* if any two distinct edges share
    48‚Üí    at most one vertex. -/
    49‚Üídef Hypergraph3Uniform.IsLinear {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą H.edges, e‚āĀ ‚Č† e‚āā ‚Üí (e‚āĀ ‚ą© e‚āā).card ‚Č§ 1
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- A set $S$ of vertices is *independent* in $H$ if no edge of $H$ is
    53‚Üí    contained in $S$. -/
    54‚Üídef Hypergraph3Uniform.IsIndependent {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n)
    55‚Üí    (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    56‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ¬¨(e ‚äÜ S)
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- The independence number of $H$: the maximum size of an independent set. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def Hypergraph3Uniform.independenceNumber {n : ‚Ąē}
    60‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n) : ‚Ąē :=
    61‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n), H.IsIndependent S ‚ąß S.card = k}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- `linearHypergraphIndNum n` is the minimum independence number over all
    64‚Üí    $3$-uniform linear hypergraphs on $n$ vertices, i.e. the largest $f$ such
    65‚Üí    that every such hypergraph has an independent set of size at least $f$. -/
    66‚Üínoncomputable def linearHypergraphIndNum (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    67‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Hypergraph3Uniform n, H.IsLinear ‚ąß
    68‚Üí    H.independenceNumber = k}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1024 (solved by Phelps‚ÄďR√∂dl [PhRo86]):
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí$f(n) \asymp (n \log n)^{1/2}$, where $f(n)$ is the minimum independence number over
    74‚Üíall $3$-uniform linear hypergraphs on $n$ vertices.
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíThere exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    77‚Üí$$C_1 \sqrt{n \log n} \leq f(n) \leq C_2 \sqrt{n \log n}.$$
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_1024 :
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C‚āĀ C‚āā : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C‚āĀ ‚ąß 0 < C‚āā ‚ąß
    82‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    83‚Üí      C‚āĀ * Real.sqrt (‚ÜĎn * Real.log ‚ÜĎn) ‚Č§ ‚ÜĎ(linearHypergraphIndNum n) ‚ąß
    84‚Üí      ‚ÜĎ(linearHypergraphIndNum n) ‚Č§ C‚āā * Real.sqrt (‚ÜĎn * Real.log ‚ÜĎn) := by
    85‚Üí  sorry
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üíend Erdos1024

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 716
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/716](https://www.erdosproblems.com/716)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\mathcal{F}$ be the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs on 6 vertices with 3 edges.
    25‚ÜíIs it true that $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}) = o(n^2)$? Answered affirmatively by
    26‚ÜíRuzsa and Szemer√©di [RuSz78].
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[BES73] Brown, W.G., ErdŇĎs, P., and S√≥s, V.T., *Some extremal problems on r-graphs*.
    29‚ÜíNew Directions in the Theory of Graphs (1973), 55-63.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[RuSz78] Ruzsa, I.Z. and Szemer√©di, E., *Triple systems with no six points carrying three
    32‚Üítriangles*. Combinatorics (1978), 939-945.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos716
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of 3-element subsets. -/
    38‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (n : ‚Ąē) where
    39‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    40‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph is $\mathcal{F}$-free (where $\mathcal{F}$ is the family of all
    43‚Üí3-uniform hypergraphs on 6 vertices with 3 edges) if every 6-element subset of
    44‚Üívertices contains at most 2 edges. -/
    45‚Üídef Hypergraph3.isFree {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = 6 ‚Üí
    47‚Üí    (H.edges.filter (¬∑ ‚äÜ S)).card ‚Č§ 2
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 716** (Brown-ErdŇĎs-S√≥s conjecture [BES73], proved by Ruzsa-Szemer√©di
    50‚Üí[RuSz78]): $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}) = o(n^2)$, i.e., for any $\varepsilon > 0$,
    51‚Üíevery sufficiently large $\mathcal{F}$-free 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices has at most
    52‚Üí$\varepsilon \cdot n^2$ edges. -/
    53‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_716 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    56‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÄ H : Hypergraph3 n, H.isFree ‚Üí
    58‚Üí    (H.edges.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ őĶ * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 := by

    61‚Üíend Erdos716

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1076
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1076](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1076)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $k \geq 5$ and let $\mathcal{F}_k$ be the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs with $k$
    25‚Üívertices and $k - 2$ edges. Is it true that $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \sim n^2 / 6$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA question of Brown, ErdŇĎs, and S√≥s [BES73] who proved this is true for $k = 4$,
    28‚Üíand that for all $k \geq 4$, $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \asymp_k n^2$.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThe asymptotic version was proved independently by Bohman and Warnke [BoWa19]
    31‚Üíand Glock, K√ľhn, Lo, and Osthus [GKLO20].
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[BES73] Brown, W.G., ErdŇĎs, P., and S√≥s, V.T., *Some extremal problems on r-graphs*, 1973.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[BoWa19] Bohman, T. and Warnke, L., 2019.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[GKLO20] Glock, S., K√ľhn, D., Lo, A., and Osthus, D., 2020.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos1076
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    45‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (n : ‚Ąē) where
    46‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    47‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph $G$ on $k$ vertices is a subhypergraph of $H$ on $n$ vertices
    50‚Üíif there is an injection from $\mathrm{Fin}(k)$ to $\mathrm{Fin}(n)$ mapping every edge of
    51‚Üí$G$ to an edge of $H$. -/
    52‚Üídef IsSubhypergraph {k n : ‚Ąē} (G : Hypergraph3 k) (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí Fin n, Function.Injective f ‚ąß
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą G.edges, e.image f ‚ąą H.edges
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- $\mathcal{F}_k$: the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs with $k$ vertices and exactly
    57‚Üí$k - 2$ edges. -/
    58‚Üídef familyF (k : ‚Ąē) : Set (Hypergraph3 k) :=
    59‚Üí  {G | G.edges.card = k - 2}
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- $H$ is $\mathcal{F}_k$-free if it contains no subhypergraph from $\mathcal{F}_k$. -/
    62‚Üídef IsFkFree {n : ‚Ąē} (k : ‚Ąē) (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    63‚Üí  ‚ąÄ G : Hypergraph3 k, G ‚ąą familyF k ‚Üí ¬¨IsSubhypergraph G H
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- The extremal number $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k)$: the maximum number of edges in an
    66‚Üí$\mathcal{F}_k$-free 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def ex3 (n k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Hypergraph3 n, IsFkFree k H ‚ąß H.edges.card = m}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1076 [BES73]:
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíFor all $k \geq 5$, $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \sim n^2 / 6$.
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíFormalized as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for sufficiently large $n$,
    76‚Üí$(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n^2 / 6 \leq \mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k)
    77‚Üí\leq (1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n^2 / 6$.
    78‚Üí-/

    80‚Üítheorem erdos_1076 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• 5 ‚Üí
    82‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    83‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    84‚Üí      (1 - őĶ) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / 6) ‚Č§ (ex3 n k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß
    85‚Üí      (ex3 n k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (1 + őĶ) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / 6) := by

    88‚Üíend Erdos1076

    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/832](https://www.erdosproblems.com/832)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r \geq 3$ and $k$ be sufficiently large in terms of $r$. Is it true that every
    25‚Üí$r$-uniform hypergraph with chromatic number $k$ has at least $\binom{(r-1)(k-1)+1}{r}$
    26‚Üíedges, with equality only for the complete graph on $(r-1)(k-1)+1$ vertices?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíWhen $r = 2$ it is a classical fact that chromatic number $k$ implies at least
    29‚Üí$\binom{k}{2}$ edges. ErdŇĎs asked for $k$ to be large since he knew the conjecture to
    30‚Üíbe false for $r = k = 3$ (Steiner triples with 7 vertices and 7 edges).
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíThis was disproved by Alon [Al85] for all $r \geq 4$. The case $r = 3$ remains open.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[Er74d] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
    35‚Üídifferences_. Mathematics of Ramsey Theory (1974).
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[Al85] Alon, N., _Hypergraphs with high chromatic number_. Graphs and Combinatorics (1985).
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos832
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on a finite vertex set $V$: a collection of
    43‚Üí$r$-element subsets of $V$. -/
    44‚Üístructure UniformHypergraph (V : Type*) [DecidableEq V] (r : ‚Ąē) where
    45‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset V)
    46‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = r
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A proper $k$-coloring of a hypergraph: a vertex coloring such that no edge
    49‚Üíis monochromatic (for every edge, not all vertices receive the same color). -/
    50‚Üídef UniformHypergraph.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] {r : ‚Ąē}
    51‚Üí    (H : UniformHypergraph V r) (k : ‚Ąē) (f : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÄ c : Fin k, ‚ąÉ v ‚ąą e, f v ‚Č† c
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/-- The chromatic number of a uniform hypergraph: the minimum number of colors

    56‚Üínoncomputable def UniformHypergraph.chromaticNumber {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]

    58‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | k ‚Č• 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí Fin k, H.IsProperColoring k f}

    61‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 832** [Er74d]:
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíFor $r \geq 3$ and $k$ sufficiently large (in terms of $r$), is it true that every
    64‚Üí$r$-uniform hypergraph with chromatic number $k$ has at least
    65‚Üí$\binom{(r-1)(k-1)+1}{r}$ edges?
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíThis was disproved by Alon [Al85] for all $r \geq 4$.

    70‚Üítheorem erdos_832 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÄ r : ‚Ąē, r ‚Č• 3 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ k‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• k‚āÄ ‚Üí

    80‚ÜíFor $k$ sufficiently large, does every $3$-uniform hypergraph with chromatic number $k$

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 834
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/834](https://www.erdosproblems.com/834)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDoes there exist a $3$-critical $3$-uniform hypergraph in which every vertex
    25‚Üíhas degree $\geq 7$? A problem of ErdŇĎs and Lov√°sz [Er74d].
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíThere are two natural interpretations of "$3$-critical":
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí1. **Transversal criticality**: the minimum transversal (hitting set) has
    30‚Üí   size $3$, and for every edge $e$ the hypergraph $H \setminus \{e\}$ has a transversal
    31‚Üí   of size $\leq 2$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí2. **Chromatic criticality**: the chromatic number is $3$, but deleting any
    34‚Üí   edge or vertex reduces the chromatic number to at most $2$.
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíLi [Li25] resolved both formulations:
    37‚Üí- In the transversal formulation, every $3$-critical $3$-uniform hypergraph
    38‚Üí  has a vertex of degree $\leq 6$ (answer: NO).
    39‚Üí- In the chromatic formulation, there exists a $3$-critical $3$-uniform
    40‚Üí  hypergraph on $9$ vertices with minimum degree $7$ (answer: YES).
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[Er74d] ErdŇĎs, P. and Lov√°sz, L. (1974).
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[Li25] Li (2025).
    45‚Üí-/
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üíopen Finset
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üínamespace Erdos834
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- A hypergraph on vertex type $V$, represented as a finset of edges,
    52‚Üíwhere each edge is a finset of vertices. -/
    53‚Üístructure Hypergraph (V : Type*) [DecidableEq V] where
    54‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset V)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is $r$-uniform if every edge has exactly $r$ vertices. -/
    57‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsUniform {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    58‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, e.card = r
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree of a vertex $v$ in a hypergraph is the number of edges containing $v$. -/
    61‚Üídef Hypergraph.degree {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  (H.edges.filter (fun e => v ‚ąą e)).card
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/-- The vertices of a hypergraph (the union of all edges). -/
    65‚Üídef Hypergraph.vertices {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) : Finset V :=
    66‚Üí  H.edges.biUnion id
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/-- The hypergraph obtained by removing an edge. -/
    69‚Üídef Hypergraph.eraseEdge {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)
    70‚Üí    (e : Finset V) : Hypergraph V :=
    71‚Üí  ‚ü®H.edges.erase e‚ü©
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/-- The hypergraph obtained by removing a vertex (and all edges containing it). -/
    74‚Üídef Hypergraph.eraseVertex {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)
    75‚Üí    (v : V) : Hypergraph V :=
    76‚Üí  ‚ü®H.edges.filter (fun e => v ‚ąČ e)‚ü©
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of a hypergraph: no edge is monochromatic. -/
    79‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)
    80‚Üí    {őĪ : Type*} (f : V ‚Üí őĪ) : Prop :=
    81‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, e.card ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ u ‚ąą e, ‚ąÉ v ‚ąą e, f u ‚Č† f v
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí/-- A set $S$ is a transversal (hitting set) of $H$ if it intersects every edge. -/
    84‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsTransversal {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)
    85‚Üí    (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    86‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÉ v ‚ąą e, v ‚ąą S
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is $3$-transversal-critical if the minimum transversal has
    89‚Üísize $3$ and removing any single edge allows a transversal of size $\leq 2$. -/
    90‚Üídef Hypergraph.Is3TransversalCritical {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    91‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph V) : Prop :=
    92‚Üí  (‚ąÉ S : Finset V, S.card = 3 ‚ąß H.IsTransversal S) ‚ąß
    93‚Üí  (‚ąÄ S : Finset V, S.card < 3 ‚Üí ¬¨H.IsTransversal S) ‚ąß
    94‚Üí  (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÉ S : Finset V, S.card ‚Č§ 2 ‚ąß (H.eraseEdge e).IsTransversal S)
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is $3$-chromatic-critical if its chromatic number is $3$ and
    97‚Üíremoving any edge or vertex makes it $2$-colorable. -/
    98‚Üídef Hypergraph.Is3ChromaticCritical {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    99‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph V) : Prop :=
   100‚Üí  (‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí Fin 3, H.IsProperColoring f) ‚ąß

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 836
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/836](https://www.erdosproblems.com/836)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r \geq 2$ and $G$ be an $r$-uniform hypergraph with chromatic number 3
    25‚Üí(that is, there is a 3-colouring of the vertices of $G$ such that no edge
    26‚Üíis monochromatic). Suppose any two edges of $G$ have a non-empty intersection.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíMust $G$ contain $O(r^2)$ many vertices?
    29‚ÜíMust there be two edges which meet in $\gg r$ many vertices?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Shelah [Er74d].
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíAlon showed the first question is false: there exists an intersecting
    34‚Üí$r$-uniform 3-chromatic hypergraph with $\sim 4^r / \sqrt{r}$ vertices.
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Lov√°sz [ErLo75] proved that there must be two edges meeting
    37‚Üíin $\Omega(r / \log r)$ vertices. The second question ($\Omega(r)$) remains open.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos836
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A hypergraph on vertex type $V$. -/
    45‚Üístructure Hypergraph (V : Type*) [DecidableEq V] where
    46‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset V)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is $r$-uniform if every edge has exactly $r$ vertices. -/
    49‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsUniform {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, e.card = r
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- The vertices of a hypergraph (the union of all edges). -/
    53‚Üídef Hypergraph.vertices {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) : Finset V :=
    54‚Üí  H.edges.biUnion id
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of a hypergraph: no edge is monochromatic. -/
    57‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V)
    58‚Üí    {őĪ : Type*} (f : V ‚Üí őĪ) : Prop :=
    59‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, e.card ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ u ‚ąą e, ‚ąÉ v ‚ąą e, f u ‚Č† f v
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- A hypergraph has chromatic number 3: it is 3-colorable but not 2-colorable. -/
    62‚Üídef Hypergraph.HasChromaticNumber3 {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) : Prop :=
    63‚Üí  (‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí Fin 3, H.IsProperColoring f) ‚ąß
    64‚Üí  (‚ąÄ f : V ‚Üí Fin 2, ¬¨H.IsProperColoring f)
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/-- A hypergraph is intersecting if any two edges share at least one vertex. -/
    67‚Üídef Hypergraph.IsIntersecting {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] (H : Hypergraph V) : Prop :=
    68‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą H.edges, (e‚āĀ ‚ą© e‚āā).Nonempty
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 836 [Er74d]:
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíFor every $r$-uniform intersecting hypergraph with chromatic number 3,
    74‚Üíthere exist two edges meeting in $\Omega(r)$ vertices.
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíFormally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $r \geq 2$ and for every
    77‚Üísuch hypergraph, there exist two distinct edges whose intersection has
    78‚Üísize $\geq C \cdot r$.
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Lov√°sz [ErLo75] proved the weaker bound $\Omega(r / \log r)$.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_836 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    84‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    85‚Üí    ‚ąÄ r : ‚Ąē, r ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí
    86‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē) (H : Hypergraph (Fin n)),
    87‚Üí      H.IsUniform r ‚Üí
